# Engineering Roadmap

_The engineering roadmap for delivery. The **Current Milestone** is first-class and detailed; past milestones are a record; future milestones are rough. This is distinct from the business narrative roadmap (`product-roadmap-template.md`): it carries milestone/validation semantics for engineering delivery, not a business storytelling view._

## Completed Milestones

| Milestone | Shipped | Outcome achieved | Links |
|---|---|---|---|

_(Empty at inception; filled as milestones ship.)_

## Current Milestone

_The milestone the team is delivering now. For a new project this is the MVP; for a legacy project this is the next ADOS-managed deliverable. NOT "MVP" for legacy — the product already exists._

### Scope

**Deliverables:**

- <deliverable>

**In scope:**

- <in-scope-item>

**Out of scope (for this milestone):**

- <out-of-scope-item>

### Success metrics (outcomes, not outputs)

_Outcome metrics that prove the milestone delivered user value. Avoid output vanity metrics (story points, feature counts) as primary success criteria._

| Metric | Definition | Target | Guardrail |
|---|---|---|---|

### Dependencies

_Upstream milestones, teams, vendors, or decisions this milestone depends on._

- <dependency>

### Validation approach

_How will we know this milestone succeeded? State the validation method (instrumented metric, cohort comparison, qualitative check) and the decision it drives (proceed, pivot, kill)._

- Method:
- Decision it drives:

### OST / discovery linkage

_When product discovery has been done, link each outcome above to the Opportunity Solution Tree. For each milestone outcome, name the opportunity and solution(s) it realises. If no discovery has been done, state "No OST — outcomes are north-star-derived assumptions" and surface them in the assumption register._

| Milestone outcome | Opportunity (OST) | Solution (OST) | Experiment |
|---|---|---|---|

## Future Milestones

_Rough, outcome-oriented placeholders. Refine as the current milestone approaches completion._

| Milestone | Outcome hypothesis | Rough timing |
|---|---|---|
